description: This API routine is obsolete. It has been replaced by the API routine ASCC_3, and is implemented by a call to ASCC_3. This API is used to apply a series of faults at various locations in the working case. This API routine was first introduced in release 32.0.0. ierr = ascc_2(sid, all, status, relfile, fcdfile, scfile)
python_syntax: ierr = ascc_2(sid, all, status, relfile, fcdfile, scfile)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: A valid subsystem identifier. valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. subsystem sid must have been previously defined. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: All buses or selected subsystem flag. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: An array of thirteen elements specifying integer options. (input).

parameters.name: relfile
parameters.description: Relay data (.rel) file. (input; blank by default).

parameters.name: fcdfile
parameters.description: Fault control (.fcd) file. (input; blank by default).

parameters.name: scfile
parameters.description: Ascc_2 results (.sc) file. (input; blank by default).

all.description: process only buses in subsystem SID.

status.description: line-to-ground faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘fltlg’.
line-out faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘linout’.
line-end faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘linend’.
flat conditions option (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘flatop’.
pre-fault conditions taken from working case.
impose flat conditions as pre-fault condition.
double-precision fault calculation option (ignored, but must be specified as 0 or 1; 0 by default; double precision always used), can also be specified with keyword ‘nouse1’.
report option (1 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rptop’.
fault contributions to N levels away.
total fault currents and fault contributions to N levels away.
number of levels back for contributions (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rptlvl’.
branch quantity code (ignored, but must be specified as 0 or 1; 0, by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘nouse2’.
dc line and FACTS device option (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘dcload’.
zero sequence transformer impedance correction option (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘zcorec’.
three phase faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘flt3ph’.
line-line-to-ground faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘fltllg’.
line-to-line faults (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘fltll’.
